response.justification: The candidate is a strong, senior full-stack engineer with impressive credentials (Google, Meta) and genuine high-performance systems experience, but the fit for this role is poor. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a bachelor thesis, against a role demanding deep, current C++/STL expertise. His low-latency work was in DLang, not C++, and the resume shows no MQTT, message brokers, industrial protocols, IEC 62443/SDL practice, or Azure. His trajectory has moved decisively toward web/TypeScript, the opposite direction from industrial C++ development.
strengths: High-performance, low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests per second
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source testing tools
Senior-level track record at top-tier companies with multi-threaded, networked service experience
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014-2015); recent decade is TypeScript/Node/DLang
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or fieldbus protocol experience whatsoever
No demonstrated message-broker architectures or Azure cloud exposure required/preferred by the role

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443 background
